findings.title: Unauthenticated user creation
findings.problem: User creation endpoint has no authentication or role check.
findings.risk: Any client can create a valid account and bypass expected access control.
findings.fix: Require authenticated privileged user and enforce role-based authorization.

findings.title: Public attachment exposure
findings.problem: Uploaded files are served publicly without passing through domain authorization.
findings.risk: Unauthorized access to pane evidence and operational files.
findings.fix: Remove public static serving and expose attachments through authenticated, authorized download endpoints.

findings.title: Weak upload validation
findings.problem: Validation is based on filename extension only, ignores MIME and file signature, and accepts .pdf despite SPECS saying jpg/png.
findings.risk: Disguised malicious files can be stored; implementation drifts from specification.
findings.fix: Validate allowed MIME types and magic bytes, reject mismatches, and align allowed formats with spec or formally update the spec.

findings.title: Pane resolution can miss resolver identity
findings.problem: A pane can reach RESOLVIDA via generic update with data_conclusao set but concluido_por_id left null.
findings.risk: Broken audit trail and incomplete rastreabilidade.
findings.fix: Force all resolution through /concluir or set concluido_por_id in every resolution path.

findings.title: Date filters not exposed in HTTP API
findings.problem: Service supports data_inicio/data_fim but router does not accept them.
findings.fix: Add date query parameters to the panes listing endpoint and test them.

findings.title: Upload route behavior depends on startup ordering
findings.problem: Upload directory is created at startup, but static mount happens earlier during app construction.
findings.risk: Clean deployments may not expose /uploads consistently.
findings.fix: Create directory before mount or remove the static serving approach.

findings.title: Inconsistent HTTP error semantics
findings.problem: 404 and 409 are mixed for not-found and business-conflict cases.
findings.risk: Client integrations become less predictable and error handling is harder.
findings.fix: Standardize 404 for missing resources, 409 for business conflicts, and 422 for invalid input.

findings.title: Implicit transaction boundary in dependency layer
findings.problem: Database dependency auto-commits at request end, hiding transaction boundaries.
findings.risk: Harder maintenance and less explicit transactional behavior.
findings.fix: Document the pattern clearly or move transaction control to a more explicit application/service layer.

findings.title: Code/spec mismatch
findings.problem: Some API behavior and comments do not match the requirement documents.
findings.fix: Align code, docs, and API payload semantics; remove or persist unused fields like observacao_conclusao.

findings.title: Critical test gaps
findings.problem: Tests do not cover some of the highest-risk behaviors found in review.
findings.risk: Security and audit regressions may pass unnoticed.
findings.fix: Add tests for protected user creation, attachment authorization, concluido_por_id consistency, date filters, and binary file validation.

verification_note: Findings are from direct code review against requirements. Test execution was attempted in the current environment but timed out.
